app.name: Splnit.eu
app.tagline: Compliance automation for EU SMB teams

dashboardBadge.title: What the {count} means
dashboardBadge.body: This is the number of unread relevant regulation updates from the last 30 days, matched to your plan and selected frameworks. It is not a control count or gap count.

shell.freePlanBanner: Free plan: explore setup with one small workspace. Upgrade only when you need higher limits for frameworks, integrations, users, or customer-facing proof.

appError.title: Something failed to load
appError.body: The event was recorded. Refresh the page or return to the dashboard.

authFallback.title: Clerk is not configured
authFallback.signInBody: Add NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY and CLERK_SECRET_KEY to the environment to enable sign-in.
authFallback.signUpBody: Add the Clerk keys to the environment to enable registration and organisations.

appDataNotice.demoBody: This page is showing local demo data because ENABLE_LOCAL_DEMO_DATA is enabled outside production. Do not treat it as organisation data.
appDataNotice.unavailableBody: Live organisation data could not be loaded. Demo data is not shown in production.

billingSettings.title: Subscription
billingSettings.subtitle: Manage the subscription for this organisation. The Stripe Price ID determines the actual billing currency and amount.
billingSettings.stripeConfigured: Billing keys and Price IDs are configured.
billingSettings.stripeMissing: Stripe keys or Price IDs are not configured in this environment yet.
billingSettings.success: Your subscription was activated. Stripe status may take a few seconds to sync.
billingSettings.canceled: Checkout was cancelled. You can start it again.
billingSettings.agencyRequired: The consultant portal requires the Agency plan.
billingSettings.portalMissing: The customer portal will be available after a Stripe customer is created.

sme.name: SME
sme.description: Full compliance workspace for one organisation.
features: Verified connectors according to availability and permissions
PDF export of the security-measures overview

agency.name: Agency
agency.description: Agency portal for managing client organisations.

billingSettings.billingUnavailable: Checkout and customer portal are disabled in this environment until Stripe keys and Price IDs are configured.

frameworkWizard.error: The assessment could not be saved.
frameworkWizard.resultBody: The result was saved to control statuses. Next, open the mapped controls, review open gaps, and attach evidence where proof is available.
frameworkWizard.intro: Answer the setup questions to create the first control baseline. Use “Partial” when work exists but is not yet documented or tested.

mfa.text: Is MFA required for all user accounts?
mfa.help: Include primary identity, administrators, and remote access.

privileged_access.help: At least quarterly or when roles change.

guest_access.text: Are external and guest accounts controlled and removed?
guest_access.help: Inactive guests should be detected and revoked.

training.text: Do employees complete regular security training?
training.help: Onboarding training and annual refreshers with records.

incident_plan.text: Do you have an approved incident response plan?
incident_plan.help: Include roles, escalation, communications, and review.

incident_reporting.text: Can you track and meet 72-hour notification deadlines?
incident_reporting.help: GDPR and NIS2 have strict incident reporting deadlines.

encryption.text: Is sensitive data encrypted at rest?

patching.text: Do you run a managed patch management process?
patching.help: Track critical vulnerabilities, deadlines, and exceptions.

backups.text: Do you test restore from backups?
backups.help: Creating backups is not enough; restore must be verified regularly.

logging.text: Do you collect security logs and alerts?
logging.help: Cover cloud, identity, endpoints, and critical applications.

suppliers.text: Do you assess supplier security and contractual requirements?
suppliers.help: NIS2 and ISO 27001 both require supply-chain controls.

asset_inventory.text: Do you maintain a current inventory of assets and systems?
asset_inventory.help: Include SaaS, cloud, endpoints, applications, and responsible people.

business_continuity.text: Do you have a documented business continuity plan?
business_continuity.help: Include responsible people, recovery objectives, and dependencies.

secure_baseline.text: Do you use secure configuration baselines for systems?
secure_baseline.help: For example hardened settings for M365, AWS, or endpoints.

network_segmentation.text: Are critical systems segmented from the network?
network_segmentation.help: Segmentation limits the impact of compromise.

change_management.text: Are changes to critical systems approved and traceable?
change_management.help: Include configuration, infrastructure, and application changes.

penetration_test.text: Do you run at least annual penetration testing or security audit?
penetration_test.help: Focus on internet-facing systems and critical processes.

endpoint_protection.text: Are endpoints protected with EDR/AV and centrally managed?
endpoint_protection.help: Cover company laptops, servers, and mobile devices.

ai_inventory.text: Do you maintain an inventory of AI systems in use?
ai_inventory.help: Record purpose, supplier, owner, data, and risk category.

ai_literacy.text: Do employees working with AI complete AI literacy training?
ai_literacy.help: Article 4 applies broadly to deployers and providers.

prohibited_practices.text: Have you checked that AI is not used for prohibited practices?
prohibited_practices.help: For example social scoring or workplace emotion recognition.

high_risk_classification.text: Do you classify whether AI falls into high-risk categories?
high_risk_classification.help: HR, education, credit, biometrics, and critical infrastructure.

provider_verification.text: Do you verify compliance of high-risk AI providers?
provider_verification.help: CE marking, EU declaration of conformity, and instructions for use.

human_oversight.text: Is human oversight defined for AI decisions?
human_oversight.help: A responsible person must be able to assess, interrupt, or reverse decisions.

ai_logs.text: Do you retain high-risk AI usage logs for at least 6 months?
ai_logs.help: Include automatic logs and manual records of use.

individual_notice.text: Do you inform affected people about AI-assisted decision-making?
individual_notice.help: Especially for HR, customer decisions, and public services.

content_labeling.text: Do you label AI-generated content where required?
content_labeling.help: Chatbots, synthetic content, and deepfake outputs.

data_governance.text: Do you have rules for input data, personal data, and sensitive information in AI?
data_governance.help: Connect AI governance with GDPR and data classification.

incident_monitoring.text: Can you detect and handle incidents or harmful AI outputs?
incident_monitoring.help: Include escalation, corrective actions, and audit trail.

supplier_contracts.text: Do AI suppliers have contractual security and compliance requirements?
supplier_contracts.help: Document roles, data, responsibilities, and termination terms.

ropa.text: Do you maintain records of personal data processing activities?

privacy_notice.text: Is your privacy notice current and understandable?
privacy_notice.help: Describe purposes, legal bases, recipients, and data subject rights.

dsr.text: Do you have a process for data subject requests?
dsr.help: Access, erasure, rectification, portability, and objection.

retention.text: Do you have a retention schedule and data deletion rules?
retention.help: Personal data should not be kept longer than necessary.

dpia.text: Do you perform DPIAs for risky processing?
dpia.help: For example monitoring, profiling, or large-scale sensitive data.

processors.text: Do processors have DPAs and security requirements in place?
processors.help: Suppliers processing personal data need contractual controls.

classification.text: Do you classify personal and sensitive data in systems?
classification.help: This helps manage access, encryption, and retention rules.

physical_access.text: Are offices and sensitive areas physically protected?
physical_access.help: Access, visitors, and physical media records.

media_disposal.text: Do you dispose of media and devices securely?
media_disposal.help: Disk wiping, shredding, and disposal records.

crypto_policy.text: Do you have a cryptography and key management policy?

code_review.text: Do you require code review and main branch protection?
code_review.help: Applies to development teams and critical repositories.

secrets.text: Do you manage secrets and detect leaks in code?
secrets.help: Secret scanning, token rotation, and least privilege.

dependency_alerts.help: Dependency alerts, SBOM, or regular vulnerability scans.

cloud_audit.text: Do you have cloud audit logs and baseline security controls?
cloud_audit.help: CloudTrail, root MFA, S3 encryption, or equivalents.

change_management_iso.help: Approval, testing, rollback, and change owner.

isms_governance.text: Have you defined ISMS scope, SoA, and a risk treatment plan?
isms_governance.help: A certification audit will expect scope, Statement of Applicability, and linkage between risks and controls.

isms_review.text: Do internal audits and management reviews take place?
isms_review.help: ISO 27001 requires a demonstrable cycle of internal audits, corrections, and management review.

document_control.text: Do you control documentation and approve exceptions?
document_control.help: Policies, procedures, exceptions, and records need an owner, version, and review.

remote_and_mobile.text: Are mobile devices and remote work security-managed?
remote_and_mobile.help: Include MDM, encryption, remote wipe, VPN, and home-working rules.

materiality.text: Have you completed double materiality and approved ESG priorities?
materiality.help: CSRD requires assessing company impacts and the financial effect of ESG topics on the company.

emissions.text: Do you measure Scope 1, Scope 2, and relevant Scope 3 emissions?
emissions.help: Each number needs a clear data source, method, and owner.

resource_use.text: Do you track energy, water, waste, and environmental incidents?
resource_use.help: Core environmental data must be auditable and regularly updated.

environmental_risk.text: Do you assess climate risks and biodiversity impacts?
environmental_risk.help: If a topic is not relevant, keep the rationale in the materiality assessment.

workforce.text: Do you have workforce, health and safety, training, and diversity data?
workforce.help: Social metrics must respect local employment and privacy limits.

supply_chain.text: Do you assess ESG risk in the supply chain?
supply_chain.help: Supplier questionnaires and due diligence help answer enterprise customers.

governance.text: Do you have business conduct policy and anti-corruption training?
governance.help: Governance covers ethics, conflicts of interest, corruption, and approvals.

report_approval.text: Is the ESG report approved with an auditable data trail?
report_approval.help: Before export, it must be clear who supplied, verified, and approved the data.

summary.setupPending: Complete the intake to see applicable controls, priority gaps, and next actions.

onboarding.title: Let's start the setup
onboarding.subtitle: Complete these steps to see applicable controls, priority gaps, and next actions.
onboarding.reassurance: You can explore the platform, but results and scores become reliable after the intake.
onboarding.demoNote: Demo mode: live data will load after the intake and first integration are connected.
stepHints: Add proof or mark a gap

stepDetails.title: Complete the intake questionnaire
stepDetails.description: A roughly 15-minute guide identifies which controls and frameworks apply to your organisation. Without this step, the readiness score is not reliable.
stepDetails.unlocks: Unlocks: frameworks, controls, and priority gaps

stepDetails.title: Activate relevant frameworks
stepDetails.description: Confirm which regulations to track based on the intake result: GDPR, NIS2, ISO 27001, or the EU AI Act.

stepDetails.title: Connect the first integration
stepDetails.description: Connect a supported integration so Splnit can read available technical signals. Other proof can be added manually as a document, screenshot, export, or note.

stepDetails.title: Add the first proof or gap
stepDetails.description: Choose one priority gap and add a document, screenshot, export, or note. If you do not have proof yet, mark the gap — that is a valid outcome.

evidenceHelper.title: What counts as proof?
evidenceHelper.body: Proof can be anything that helps show a process exists or a risk is managed: a document, screenshot, export, contract, system setting, user list, training record, or short note.
evidenceHelper.missing: If you do not have proof, mark the gap. Splnit should show what is missing — not pretend the control is complete.

activation.rankedGapsBody: {count} priority items are available from intake and current evidence.
activation.noRankedGaps: No ranked gap is available yet. Finish intake or review the full control focus.
activation.automationOutcomeBody: This reflects existing connector evidence and remediation task state; no new task is created from the dashboard view.

complete_intake.title: Complete intake
complete_intake.body: Finish intake to derive ranked gaps and the first evidence path.

connect_recommended_integration.title: Connect {target}
connect_recommended_integration.body: Start with {target}; it can create the first automated evidence for the highest-priority gaps.

open_recommended_workspace.title: Open {target}
open_recommended_workspace.body: Use the {target} workspace to review the first system-specific controls and evidence.

upload_first_evidence.title: Add first evidence for {target}
upload_first_evidence.body: Upload a screenshot, export, document, or note for the first ranked gap. If proof is missing, mark the gap honestly.

review_first_gap.title: Review {target}
review_first_gap.body: The first evidence-backed gap needs human review before it changes the readiness story.

active_monitoring.title: Continue priority controls
active_monitoring.body: The first activation loop is running. Keep working through the ranked controls and evidence.

review_ranked_gaps.title: Review ranked gaps
review_ranked_gaps.body: No single next evidence target is available yet. Review the ranked control focus and choose the first honest gap.

metrics.scoreBody: Indicative summary of framework and control states based on documented inputs.
metrics.scorePendingBody: Available after intake and first control or proof assessments.

frameworkDescriptions.nis2: Cybersecurity, risk management, incident reporting, and management accountability.
frameworkDescriptions.ai-act: Obligations for AI deployers, AI literacy, high-risk AI, and transparency.
frameworkDescriptions.gdpr: Personal data protection, processing records, security, and breach notification.
frameworkDescriptions.iso27001: Information security management system and security control catalogue.
frameworkDescriptions.csrd: ESG reporting, double materiality, data evidence, and supplier questionnaires.

nukibMethodology.title: Example guidance update
nukibMethodology.summary: Sample recommendation for managing supplier risk in a regulated programme.

nukibCve.title: Example CVE exposure review
nukibCve.summary: Sample vulnerability item that would require an exposure check.

uoouGuidance.title: Example GDPR guidance
uoouGuidance.summary: Sample guidance item for processing records and security measures.

intakeScope.title: 15-minute intake scope
intakeScope.subtitle: Deterministic readiness scope from onboarding answers. Internal guidance only, not a legal classification.
intakeScope.priority: Priority gaps based on your intake

evidence.title: Evidence vault
evidence.subtitle: Storage for manual uploads and automated snapshots linked to controls.

records.title: Evidence records
records.empty: Evidence will appear after framework setup, integrations, uploads, or generated documents create the first control records.
records.emptyAutomationOutcome: A connector automation state already exists for a priority control, but no downloadable evidence record is available yet. Review the affected control before creating manual proof.
records.emptyFiltered: No evidence records match the current filters. Clear or adjust filters to see other records.

descriptions.nis2: Cybersecurity, risk management, incident reporting, and management accountability.
descriptions.ai-act: Obligations for AI deployers, AI literacy, high-risk AI, and transparency.
descriptions.gdpr: Personal data protection, processing records, security, and breach notification.
descriptions.iso27001: Information security management system and security control catalogue.
descriptions.csrd: ESG reporting, double materiality, data evidence, and supplier questionnaires.

index.title: Regulations and standards
index.subtitle: Choose a framework, run an assessment, and track mapped control status.
index.enrolledSubtitle: Frameworks currently active in this organisation.
index.availableSubtitle: Library frameworks are shown as setup options, not active organisation scope.
index.emptyEnrolled: Start by activating one framework. NIS2 is the usual first baseline for EU cybersecurity readiness.

detail.gapReportEmpty: The PDF will be created after the first framework assessment.
detail.smartDocumentsBody: The document is pre-filled with your platform data. Review the content before sharing it with auditors.
detail.isoPackageBody: The ZIP export includes the Statement of Applicability, completed controls, available evidence, active policies, and certification body links.
detail.csrdQuestionnaireBody: Default question set for suppliers and enterprise customers.

providers.microsoft365: MFA, Conditional Access, guests, privileged roles, and sensitivity labels.
providers.github: Branch protection, secret scanning, 2FA enforcement, and dependency alerts.
providers.aws: EC2, security groups, S3 backups, and CloudTrail through a read-only IAM key.
providers.hetzner: Servers, firewalls, and snapshots in Hetzner Cloud through a read-only API token.
providers.ovhcloud: Dedicated servers, firewall, and backup storage in OVHcloud through read-only API keys.
providers.abra-flexi: REST API connection for ABRA Flexi users, transport, configuration, and manual backup fallback.
providers.google_workspace: Workspace audit logs, Drive sharing, and administrator accounts.
providers.pohoda: Workspace/checklist for Pohoda users covering data backups, mServer/API access, user roles, and manual evidence.
providers.money_s3: Workspace/checklist for Money S3/S4 users covering database backups, access, SQL Server settings, and manual evidence.
providers.helios: Workspace/checklist for Helios users covering SQL Server backups, access, MES/SCADA or EDI dependencies, and manual evidence.

index.title: Automated tests
index.subtitle: Connected services provide evidence and update control statuses.
index.plannedCardNote: Planned. Production checks are not enabled yet.
index.recommendationBody: Start with the integration your intake says you probably already use. Splnit will turn it into the first automated checks and evidence; if your stack is not listed, use manual upload below.
index.resultsAria: Results over 24 hours: pass, fail, warning, manual review, error
index.stackMissingBody: Manual upload keeps the activation loop usable: choose a priority control, upload a screenshot, export, or document, and return to integrations later.

planned.statusBody: OAuth flow and token storage for this provider are not active yet.
planned.plannedChecksBody: These checks are not connected to automated run history yet.
planned.dependencyBody: Add this connector after the current provider set has been validated.
planned.scopeItemStatus: Planned. Production checks are not enabled yet.

googleWorkspace.description: Google Workspace audit logs, Drive sharing, and administrator account checks are planned after the current connector set is validated.
checks: Admin MFA and privileged account review

common.runResultsEmpty: Results will appear after the first automated run.
common.connectionUnavailable: Connection actions are disabled until app credentials and an active workspace are available. The test scope below is still visible for review.

aws.title: AWS integration
aws.subtitle: A read-only IAM access key checks the baseline state of EC2, security groups, S3 backups, and CloudTrail.
aws.testsBody: AWS workspace checks will run after the key is connected.
aws.permissionsBody: Use a dedicated IAM user with a read-only policy. The secret access key is encrypted at rest and is never displayed back.

errors.validation: Check the Access Key ID, Secret Access Key, region, and optional S3 bucket details.
errors.invalid_key: AWS rejected the IAM key. Enter a new or corrected key.
errors.insufficient_scope: The IAM key does not have the minimum required read permissions.
errors.unreachable: AWS could not be reached. Try again or provide manual evidence.

hetzner.title: Hetzner Cloud integration
hetzner.subtitle: A read-only API token checks running servers, firewall rules, and recent snapshots.
hetzner.testsBody: Hetzner Cloud checks run after the API token is connected.
hetzner.permissionsBody: Use a read-only project token. The token is encrypted at rest and is never displayed back.

errors.invalid_key: Hetzner Cloud rejected the API token. Enter a new or corrected token.
errors.insufficient_scope: The API token does not have the minimum required read permissions.
errors.unreachable: Hetzner Cloud could not be reached. Try again or provide manual evidence.

ovhcloud.title: OVHcloud integration
ovhcloud.subtitle: Read-only API keys check the dedicated server, firewall, and backup storage.
ovhcloud.testsBody: OVHcloud checks run after the application and consumer keys are connected.
ovhcloud.permissionsBody: Use read-only rules for dedicated servers, firewall, and backup storage. Secret values are encrypted at rest.

errors.validation: Check the application key, application secret, consumer key, and optional service name.
errors.invalid_key: OVHcloud rejected the API keys. Enter new or corrected values.
errors.insufficient_scope: The API keys do not have the minimum required read permissions.
errors.unreachable: OVHcloud could not be reached. Try again or provide manual evidence.

github.title: GitHub integration
github.subtitle: A read-only GitHub App checks 2FA, branch protection, secret scanning, dependency alerts, and code scanning.
github.repositoriesEmpty: Repositories will appear after the GitHub App is installed.
github.testsBody: Mapped to MFA, code review, secrets, and dependency controls.

microsoft365.title: Microsoft 365 integration
microsoft365.subtitle: OAuth connection runs checks for MFA, Conditional Access, guests, privileged roles, sensitivity labels, and training.
microsoft365.testsBody: Mapped to NIS2, ISO 27001, and GDPR controls.

discoveryHandoff.draftsTitle: You have {count} Microsoft 365 drafts to review
discoveryHandoff.draftsBody: Auto-discovery prepared {assets} assets and {vendors} suppliers. Review them before they affect your register and evidence. This is separate from the older automated-test sync, so “last sync” may still be empty.
discoveryHandoff.zeroBody: Microsoft 365 is connected and discovery ran, but we did not find new assets, suppliers, or risky apps waiting for confirmation.
discoveryHandoff.errorTitle: Discovery result could not be loaded
discoveryHandoff.errorBody: The connector is active, but Splnit cannot show draft counts right now. You can continue and refresh later.
discoveryHandoff.boundaryBody: Drafts stay in staging; register rows and evidence are created only after your decision.

incidents.title: Incidents
incidents.subtitle: NIS2 and GDPR incident log with 72-hour deadline tracking, regulatory checklists, and notification exports.

demo.title: Suspicious admin access
demo.description: Demo incident covering unauthorized access to a SaaS admin panel and potential personal data exposure.

wizard.title: Incident wizard
wizard.name: Name
wizard.affectsCriticalSystems: Incident affects critical systems or services

detail.selectOrCreate: Select an incident from the log or create a new one.

checklist.title: Regulatory checklist
checklist.nis2Required: Requires impact assessment and notification decision.
checklist.nis2NotMarked: Not marked as a critical system.

risks.title: Risks
risks.subtitle: ISO 27001 risk register with likelihood × impact scoring, owner, due date, and mitigation status.

seed.title: Common SMB risks
seed.body: Add 10 common SMB risks when the register is empty.

register.title: Register
register.empty: The register is empty. Add a risk or seed common risks.

demoRisks.title: MFA is not enforced on key accounts
demoRisks.description: MFA is not enforced for every cloud and administrator account.

demoRisks.title: Backup recovery is not verified
demoRisks.description: Backups are not tested regularly and recovery may not meet RTO.

demoRisks.title: Supplier security is not managed
demoRisks.description: A critical SaaS supplier has no security assessment or DPA on file.

demoRisks.title: Incident response is not ready
demoRisks.description: Incident response roles are not rehearsed and reporting could exceed 72h.

demoRisks.title: Personal data retention is unmanaged
demoRisks.description: Personal data is stored in tools without clear retention and access rules.

demoRisks.title: Endpoint management is weak
demoRisks.description: Laptops do not have consistent encryption, EDR, and patch management.

demoRisks.title: Access reviews are irregular
demoRisks.description: Access rights for former employees and suppliers are not reviewed consistently.

demoRisks.title: AI tool usage is uncontrolled
demoRisks.description: Employees use AI tools without rules for sensitive data.

demoRisks.title: Audit trail coverage is insufficient
demoRisks.description: Audit logs are not centralised or do not have enough retention.

demoRisks.title: Security training evidence is missing
demoRisks.description: Security training cannot be evidenced for every employee.

clientsPage.title: Client dashboard
clientsPage.subtitle: Manage client organisations, monitor scores, and prepare white-label Trust Center outputs.

lock.demoBody: Clerk or the database is not available, so the form is locked and the page uses demo client data.
lock.planBody: Production client organisation linking is available only on the Agency plan.

list.title: Clients
list.empty: You do not have any connected clients yet.

signup.headline: Launch your consultant portal
signup.subtitle: Create the agency profile and continue to the Agency plan payment.
signup.canceled: Checkout was cancelled. Your details are ready to try again.

form.name: Agency name
form.duplicateSlug: This slug is already used by another agency.
form.invalid: Check the agency name and slug.
form.genericError: Agency signup could not be started.

signupComplete.title: Setting up your portal
signupComplete.body: Waiting for Stripe payment confirmation and agency creation.
signupComplete.timeout: Setup is taking longer than usual. Refresh this page in a moment.

dashboard.title: Managed clients
dashboard.subtitle: Review client scores, open gaps, and recent activity across organisations linked to this agency.
dashboard.empty: No linked clients match these filters.

client.noPriorityControls: No priority controls are currently open for this client.

banner.title: Consultant read-only mode
banner.body: You can review evidence state and leave comments for this client. Attestations, file uploads, and connector activation remain disabled for consultants.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage agency branding, client links, and consultant access.

clients.copyManually: Copy this path and send it through your approved channel.
clients.empty: No client organisations are linked yet.

consultants.deliveryNote: Email delivery is recorded as pending until Clerk invitation wiring is enabled.

invites.title: Connect this organisation
invites.body: {agency} invited this organisation to be managed in the agency portal.
invites.consultantBody: {agency} invited you as a consultant in the agency portal.
invites.invalidBody: This invite could not be found.
invites.activeHelp: Accepting links your active Clerk organisation to the agency. Switch organisation first if needed.
invites.consultantHelp: Accepting adds your user account to the agency consultants.
invites.unavailable: This invite is no longer available.

workspace.emptyLayers: No layers configured for this workspace.

readOnly.title: Read-only consultant view
readOnly.body: Evidence-changing controls are disabled. Use comments to guide the client.
readOnly.controlNotice: Read-only mode: consultants can comment, but cannot submit attestations or upload evidence.

comments.title: Comments
comments.placeholder: Write a comment for this control

workspace.platformGuide: Compliance guide for {platform} — review four layers (infrastructure, access, backups, API) and attach evidence here.
workspace.demoNotice: Demo — sign in with an organisation to save real evidence
workspace.canAttachFile: You can also attach a supporting file:

attestation.saved: Attestation saved. Reload to see updated status.
attestation.notesPlaceholder: Document your approach, steps taken, or caveats...

abraFlexiWorkspace.title: ABRA Flexi
abraFlexiWorkspace.subtitle: ABRA Flexi workspace for infrastructure, access, backups, and REST API controls mapped to ZoKB and NIS2.
abraFlexiWorkspace.demoMode: Demo — sign in with an organisation to save a real connection.

connection.title: Connect ABRA Flexi
connection.body: Enter the instance URL, company identifier, and a dedicated REST API user. Password and username are stored encrypted.

errors.validation: Check the URL, company identifier, and credentials.
errors.insufficient_scope: The REST API user lacks the required read permissions.
errors.unreachable: ABRA Flexi could not be reached.

teamPage.title: Access and training
teamPage.subtitle: Access reviews, role ownership, and training evidence in one place.

accessReviews.title: Access reviews
accessReviews.description: Quarterly keep, revoke, or modify workflow for Microsoft 365 and GitHub users.

roleAssignments.title: Role assignments
roleAssignments.description: Role owners, administrators, and contractor access inventory.

trainingLog.title: Training log
trainingLog.description: Security and AI literacy attendance evidence for auditors.

trainingPage.title: §5 training tracker
trainingPage.subtitle: Organisation-scoped evidence for security, role-based, incident, privacy, and AI training tied to ctrl_security_training_annual.
trainingPage.demoMode: Demo — sign in with an organisation to save real records.

table.title: Training records
table.empty: No training records have been saved yet.

trainingPage.coverageNote: Coverage against the total employee count is not calculated yet because the organisation profile stores team size as a band, not an exact number.

vendorsPage.title: Vendor risk
vendorsPage.subtitle: Vendor catalogue, risk-tier assessment, and NIS2 supply-chain report.
vendorsPage.smartDocumentNotice: The document is pre-filled with your platform data. Review the content before sharing it with auditors.

catalog.title: Catalogue
catalog.empty: No vendors have been added yet.

questionNotes.data_processing: This answer captures inherent data-processing exposure. It is not, by itself, a maturity failure.

questions.security_owner: Does the vendor have a named security owner?
questions.iso_certification: Does the vendor have ISO 27001, SOC 2, or an equivalent audit?
questions.data_processing: Does the vendor process personal or sensitive data?
questions.subprocessors: Does the vendor provide a sub-processor list?
questions.incident_notice: Does the contract require security incident notification to the customer?
questions.access_control: Does the vendor use MFA and regular access reviews?
questions.encryption: Does the vendor encrypt data in transit and at rest?
questions.backup_recovery: Does the vendor test backups and disaster recovery?
questions.vulnerability_management: Does the vendor have a vulnerability management process?
questions.business_continuity: Does the vendor have a business continuity plan?
questions.data_location: Does the vendor keep data in the EU/EEA or under a valid transfer mechanism?
questions.termination: Can the vendor securely return or delete data after contract termination?
questions.nis2_isms: Does the supplier run an information security management system (e.g. ISO 27001 or equivalent)?
questions.nis2_management: Has the supplier's management appointed a person responsible for cybersecurity?
questions.nis2_hr_security: Does the supplier train staff in cybersecurity and manage joiner/leaver security?
questions.nis2_continuity: Does the supplier have a business continuity plan and test restore from backups?
questions.nis2_access_control: Does the supplier manage access on a least-privilege basis?
questions.nis2_identity_mfa: Does the supplier use multi-factor authentication and regular account and permission reviews?
questions.nis2_detection_logging: Does the supplier log and evaluate security events (logging, detection)?
questions.nis2_incident_response: Does the supplier have an incident response process including customer notification?
questions.nis2_network_security: Does the supplier secure its networks (segmentation, firewall, secured remote access)?
questions.nis2_application_security: Does the supplier keep applications updated and remediate known vulnerabilities (patch management)?
questions.nis2_cryptography: Does the supplier encrypt data in transit and at rest using current cryptographic algorithms?
questions.nis2_incident_impact: Does the supplier classify incident impact and inform customers about incidents affecting their services?
questions.nis2_security_policy: Does the supplier maintain documented, regularly reviewed security policies?
questions.nis2_risk_management: Does the supplier perform formal cybersecurity risk assessments?
questions.nis2_asset_management: Does the supplier keep an inventory and classification of assets (systems and data)?
questions.nis2_supplier_chain: Does the supplier manage the security of its own suppliers (sub-supplier chain)?
questions.nis2_change_management: Does the supplier have a change management process for systems and services?
questions.nis2_vulnerability_disclosure: Does the supplier manage vulnerabilities, including a reporting/disclosure channel?
questions.nis2_pentest: Does the supplier run regular penetration tests or technical audits?
questions.nis2_physical_security: Does the supplier ensure physical security of premises and infrastructure?
questions.nis2_secure_development: Does the supplier develop software following secure development practices (secure SDLC)?
questions.nis2_audit_certification: Does the supplier undergo independent security audits (ISO 27001, SOC 2)?
questions.nis2_privileged_access: Does the supplier manage privileged (admin) access separately with elevated protection?
questions.nis2_data_segregation: Does the supplier segregate individual customers' data (multi-tenant isolation)?
questions.nis2_recovery_objectives: Does the supplier have defined and tested recovery objectives (RTO/RPO)?
questions.nis2_monitoring: Does the supplier provide continuous security monitoring (SOC)?
questions.nis2_subprocessor_notice: Does the supplier give advance notice of subprocessor changes?
questions.nis2_data_location: Does the supplier keep data in the EU/EEA or under a legally covered transfer regime?
questions.nis2_exit_strategy: Can the supplier securely return or delete data after contract termination?
questions.nis2_incident_history: Has the supplier had a serious security incident in the last 24 months?

templates.nis2_lower: NIS2 — Czech lower-obligations regime (vyhláška č. 410/2025 Sb.)
templates.nis2_higher: NIS2 — Czech higher-obligations regime (vyhláška č. 409/2025 Sb.)

footer.tagline: Early access platform for EU compliance automation.

cookies.title: Cookies

earlyAccess.title: Looking for our first 10 design partners.
earlyAccess.body: Splnit.eu is in early access. It is a founder-led platform built in Olomouc for companies that want to shape the product before public launch.
earlyAccess.emptyProof: No public references are listed yet. When they exist, they will go on the site only with the customer's permission.
earlyAccess.onboardingTitle: Three weeks on a real project.
earlyAccess.onboardingBody: The goal is not a demo account. The goal is to use Splnit.eu on a real NIS2 or GDPR problem and find what the product still misses.

benefits.businessFree: 12 months free on the SME plan
benefits.guidedOnboarding: 3 weeks of guided onboarding directly with the founder
benefits.directContact: Direct contact without a ticket queue

asks.realUse: Real use on your NIS2 or GDPR project
asks.weeklyFeedback: 30 minutes of weekly feedback during onboarding
asks.referencePermission: Permission to use your name as a reference only when you are happy

weeks.week1Body: We confirm jurisdiction, walk through NIS2 applicability, and prepare the first priority list.
weeks.week2Body: We configure verified integrations or ERP workspaces according to availability and permissions, then capture the first evidence.
weeks.week3Body: We generate the first document set and prepare an evidence format for your auditor.

about.title: Who's behind Splnit.eu.
about.body: Splnit.eu is a focused, founder-led platform built by a senior IT engineer with hands-on experience in European compliance workflows.
about.notYetTitle: What we do not claim yet
about.whyP1: Large global compliance platforms often cover generic frameworks well, but EU SMB teams also need local transpositions, clear citations, and practical onboarding.
about.whyP2: Splnit.eu starts smaller: automated checks for systems companies already use, documents ready for legal review, and direct founder work with the first design partners.

realToday.solo: Splnit.eu is a founder-led early access product built in Olomouc.
realToday.focus: The product focuses on EU compliance automation for NIS2, GDPR, and ISO 27001.
realToday.legalReview: The DPA and final legal documents are prepared as working drafts for counsel review before paid production reliance.
realToday.noFakeProof: Public references and metrics are not shown until they are verified.

notYet.legalAdvice: This is not legal advice, an audit opinion, or certification.
notYet.enterprise: Splnit.eu is not yet an enterprise vendor with a 24/7 SLA and penalties.
notYet.certification: ISO 27001 or SOC 2 certification for Splnit.eu itself is not publicly claimed.
notYet.entity: Operator identification details are published on the legal pages before any contract is signed.

home.badge: EU compliance readiness for European SMBs
home.titleLine1: Make compliance work clearer in your jurisdiction.
home.lead: NIS2, EU AI Act, GDPR, and ISO 27001 workflows with regulatory citations for review. Built for European SMBs in Italian, Czech, and English.
home.trustLine: Early access · Direct founder contact · Vercel + Neon infrastructure
home.stepsTitle: Start with the core setup, then keep improving.
home.stepsBody: Three steps from first system connection to a usable evidence pack.
home.featuresTitle: Compliance built for IT and compliance teams.
home.featuresBody: Connect local and global infrastructure. Controls are mapped to local and international standards.
home.offerBody: Splnit.eu is in early access. The first design partners get the SME plan free for 12 months and three weeks of guided onboarding directly with the founder.
home.referencePlaceholder: Customer references will be published with explicit consent as design partners complete onboarding.
home.finalBody: Become a design partner and help build the product around a real NIS2 or GDPR project.

steps.connectBody: Use verified integrations or Czech ERP workspaces according to availability and permissions. Unsupported sources can be tracked manually for now.
steps.testsTitle: Checks can run on connected systems
steps.testsBody: Configured checks monitor available connected systems and save results as internal evidence for review.
steps.resultsBody: Dashboard, failure alerts, and working audit documentation for review with your advisor or auditor.

features.residencyBody: Production uses Vercel for hosting and Neon Postgres for the database. Sub-processors and processing context are listed in the DPA overview.
features.monitoringBody: Surface configuration drift in connected systems so the team can review it.
features.documentsBody: Export a Cybersecurity Assessment Report — mapped to Czech ZoKB obligations and prepared for advisor or authority review. Automatically verified controls appear as green blocks, manually declared ones as grey.
features.templatesBody: Core policies, ROPA, BCP, DPIA, and GAP analyses available as DOCX and XLSX. No registration, available to everyone.
features.smartDocumentsBody: The platform generates GAP analysis, Statement of Applicability, and vendor reports directly from your live data — not from an empty spreadsheet.
features.clientAccessBody: Share compliance posture with each customer separately. Dedicated link, dedicated regulation selection, open tracking.

leadCapture.title: Which EU regulations may matter for your company?
leadCapture.subtitle: Select your company profile — we'll highlight which EU regulations may be relevant.
leadCapture.success: Sent. Your overview will arrive shortly.
leadCapture.error: The email could not be saved. Please try again.
leadCapture.footnote: No credit card · Preview results · Unsubscribe anytime

pricing.title: Transparent pricing. No surprises.
pricing.subtitle: Monthly pricing matches the current Stripe plans used by production billing.
pricing.footnote: Billed through Stripe · Monthly subscription · Plan changes in the app
pricing.partnerBody: The Agency plan adds client workspaces, white-label outputs, and consultant management.
pricing.comparisonDocumentsNote: Core templates are free for everyone on the regulation pages. Generating pre-filled documents from platform data is included in paid plans.
pricing.finalTitle: Start free, then upgrade in the app.
pricing.finalBody: The Free plan is enough for first orientation. When you need the full workspace or agency portal, move to a paid Stripe plan.

sme.name: SME
sme.description: Full compliance workspace for one organisation.
features: Verified connectors according to availability and permissions

agency.name: Agency
agency.description: Agency portal for managing client organisations.

faqs.cancelQuestion: Can I change or stop a monthly plan?
faqs.cancelAnswer: Monthly plans can be changed by request. Annual plan changes and any refunds are handled under the agreed early-access terms.
faqs.dataQuestion: Is my data stored in the EU?
faqs.dataAnswer: The production setup is designed for EU regions. Exact processing locations and any transfer mechanisms outside the EU/EEA are confirmed in the DPA and sub-processor list.
faqs.setupQuestion: Do I need technical knowledge to set it up?
faqs.setupAnswer: The setup wizard is guided. Timing depends on your systems, permissions, and evidence sources.
faqs.legalAnswer: No. The platform helps you prepare for legal review; it does not replace a qualified lawyer.
faqs.discountQuestion: Do you offer discounts for non-profits or startups?
faqs.discountAnswer: Yes. Contact hello@splnit.eu for special terms.
faqs.cancelDataQuestion: What happens to my data after cancellation?
faqs.cancelDataAnswer: You have 30 days to export all data. After that, it is permanently deleted.

metadata.title: Comparison | Splnit.eu vs regfor.cz, cybreg.cz, Vanta/Drata, and Excel
metadata.description: Comparison of Splnit.eu with regfor.cz, cybreg.cz, Vanta/Drata, and manual Excel workflows for Czech NIS2, GDPR, and ISO 27001 work.

compare.title: Where Splnit.eu makes sense next to general GRC tools.
compare.subtitle: This page compares publicly verifiable capabilities and real product features only. For competitors, missing public evidence is labelled as such instead of being treated as proof that a feature does not exist.
compare.evidenceNote: Competitor status is based on public product pages reviewed in May 2026. Where public evidence is missing, we say “not publicly documented” instead of claiming the feature is absent.
compare.whyTitle: Splnit.eu is built for Czech regulation and accounting reality.
compare.whyBody: The difference is not generic GRC tracking. It is the combination of Czech NIS2 outputs, local ERP workspaces, CZK self-serve pricing, and a client portal for advisors.
proofPoints: Pohoda, Money S3, Helios, and ABRA Flexi are first-class product scope.
Lower-obligation outputs are mapped to Decree No. 410/2025 Coll.
Pricing is monthly, in CZK, and tied to production Stripe plans.
DOCX/XLSX templates are available without registration.

blog.title: EU compliance guides for Czech companies
blog.subtitle: Practical articles about NIS2, GDPR, the EU AI Act, and ISO 27001.
blog.empty: No articles have been published yet.

metadata.title: Agency plan for MSPs and compliance advisors | Splnit.eu
metadata.description: Splnit.eu Agency plan for MSPs, advisors, and agencies managing compliance across multiple client workspaces.

partners.title: Manage client compliance workspaces from one portal.
partners.subtitle: The Agency plan is for advisory and MSP teams that need separate client organisations, white-label outputs, and client invites without copying evidence by hand.
partners.planSummary: Up to {clients} client organisations, unlimited consultants, and white-label branding for the client portal.

benefits.title: Separate client organisations
benefits.body: Each client has its own workspace, regulation status, evidence, vendors, incidents, and exports. Advisors see the portfolio from the agency dashboard.

benefits.title: White-label branding
benefits.body: Agency settings support logo, display name, colours, and client invites so the portal fits the advisory service.

benefits.title: Client and consultant invites
benefits.body: Client invites and consultant roles support collaboration without sharing one account or sending files manually.

benefits.title: Evidence and exports in one flow
benefits.body: The same scope as the SME plan: frameworks, controls, evidence, templates, and exports for customer or regulatory proof.

partners.workflowTitle: The agency creates clients, the client fills data, Splnit.eu keeps the evidence trail.
partners.workflowBody: This page describes the existing Agency portal. It does not claim certifications, customers, or integration capabilities that are not in the product.
workflow: Create an Agency workspace and set advisory brand settings.
Invite a client organisation or create the client workspace yourself.
The client completes intake and you select relevant frameworks.
Evidence, tasks, incidents, and exports stay separated by client.

regulations.title: Which EU regulations may matter for your company?
regulations.subtitle: A plain-language starting point for EU obligations, with deadlines, enforcement risk, and practical next steps. It is not a legal classification.
regulations.downloadSubtitle: Indicative overviews, draft templates, and checklists for review.

dora.description: Digital operational resilience for financial institutions.

timeline.title: Remaining AI systems embedded in products

detail.metadataDescription: {name}: plain-language overview of obligations, sanctions, and next steps for SMBs.
detail.jsonLdDescription: {name}: obligations overview and compliance automation in Splnit.eu.
detail.appliesTitle: Indicative practical impact for EU SMBs.
detail.obligationsTitle: What often needs evidence or review.
detail.finesTitle: Risk is measured in money, contracts, and lost trust.
detail.helpsTitle: Turn obligations into controls, evidence, and deadlines.
detail.relatedTitle: If you are working on {name}, these areas probably overlap too.

onboarding.title: Organisation setup
onboarding.description: Six steps prepare the workspace: company profile, business reality, tools in use, automatically determined frameworks, first integration, and initial score.
onboarding.saveStepError: The step could not be saved.

frameworks.title: Choose frameworks

descriptions.nis2: Cybersecurity, risk management, incident reporting, and management accountability.
descriptions.ai-act: AI deployer obligations, AI literacy, high-risk AI, and transparency requirements.
descriptions.gdpr: Personal data protection, records of processing activities, security, and breach notification.
descriptions.iso27001: Information security management system and security control catalogue.
descriptions.csrd: ESG reporting, double materiality, data evidence, and supplier questionnaires.

frameworks.body: Pick the obligations Splnit.eu should turn into controls. For an Italian prospect, start with NIS2 for cybersecurity readiness or ISO 27001 if certification preparation is the immediate goal.
frameworks.nextAction: Next: save the selected baseline, then list the systems you actually use.

tools.title: AI tools and SaaS inventory
tools.body: Select the systems you actually use today. This keeps the setup grounded in your environment and avoids demo or fixture data.
tools.nextAction: Next: Splnit.eu recommends the first integration from the selected tools.

integration.title: Recommended integration
integration.body: For many NIS2 and ISO 27001 starts, Microsoft 365 is the first useful evidence source: MFA, Conditional Access, guests, and privileged roles.
integration.optional: This is a recommended next action, not a blocker. Review the scope now; connect it when the workspace owner is ready.
integration.nextAction: Next: review the initial baseline score and finish onboarding.

score.label: Initial score
score.title: The first baseline is ready
score.body: The score is an initial baseline from selected frameworks and tool inventory. It is not a compliance claim; connected integrations and reviewed evidence make it stronger.
score.nextStep: After finishing, start on the dashboard: review active frameworks, open controls, and the recommended integration step.

intake.title: 15-minute scope intake
intake.body: Answer deterministic scoping questions so Splnit.eu can prioritise the initial control baseline without making legal-compliance claims.
intake.nextAction: Next: save the derived scope, then review the recommended first integration.
intake.previewDisclaimer: This is an internal readiness scope, not a legal classification or compliance guarantee.

businessModel.label: Which business model best fits this organisation?
businessModel.helpText: Used only for readiness scoping; it is not a legal classification.

sector.label: Which sector is closest?
sector.helpText: Conservative sector signal for priority controls.

employeeBand.label: How many people work in the organisation?
employeeBand.helpText: Broad band only; no exact headcount is needed.

handlesPersonalData.label: What personal data does the organisation handle?
handlesPersonalData.helpText: This drives GDPR readiness tasks and privacy evidence priorities.

handlesSensitiveData.label: Does it handle sensitive, health, financial, or high-risk data?
handlesSensitiveData.helpText: A yes answer adds conservative privacy and encryption review tasks.

usesCloudHosting.label: Does it use cloud hosting or object storage for business systems?
usesCloudHosting.helpText: Cloud use adds logging, encryption, and root-account controls.

hasPublicApp.label: Does it operate a public app, portal, API, or customer-facing system?
hasPublicApp.helpText: Public exposure adds vulnerability and testing priorities.

hasProductionSoftware.label: Does it build or deploy production software?
hasProductionSoftware.helpText: Software delivery adds code review, dependency, and change controls.

hasCriticalOperations.label: Would downtime materially disrupt customers, patients, or production?
hasCriticalOperations.helpText: Critical operations add continuity, recovery, and segmentation priorities.

usesThirdPartyProcessors.label: How much does it rely on external processors or critical vendors?
usesThirdPartyProcessors.helpText: Vendor reliance drives supplier review and contract controls.

usesAiSystems.label: How are AI systems used?
usesAiSystems.helpText: AI answers scope governance tasks; AI does not decide legal applicability.

usesHighRiskAi.label: Could any AI use affect people in health, employment, credit, education, or access to services?
usesHighRiskAi.helpText: A yes answer adds manual review tasks for possible high-risk AI use.

accountingPlatform.label: Which accounting software does the organisation use?
accountingPlatform.helpText: Selecting a supported ERP enables a workspace recommendation and NIS2 control set for that system.

handlesPersonalData.none: No personal data beyond business contacts

organisationSettings.title: Organisation
organisationSettings.description: Basic organisation identity, classification, and Clerk organisation link.

profile.title: Organisation profile
profile.icoTitle: Company ID must contain exactly 8 digits.
profile.dicTitle: VAT ID must use the CZ12345678 format.

nis2.title: NIS2 classification
nis2.description: Indicative internal label based on sector and size. Final legal assessment stays outside automated classification.

categories.essential_or_important_entity_review: Essential or important entity review needed

questionnairePage.title: Security questionnaires
questionnairePage.subtitle: Draft answers to customer questionnaires using passing controls, evidence, and policies already stored in Splnit.eu.
questionnairePage.disclaimer: Generated answers are drafts for legal and compliance review. They are not legal advice, certification, or a guarantee of compliance.
questionnairePage.disabledNotice: AI questionnaires require Clerk, DATABASE_URL, QUESTIONNAIRE_AI_ENABLED=true, and a configured AI provider.

metrics.controlsBody: Used as the primary answer context.
metrics.evidenceBody: Automated evidence can support high confidence.

history.title: Saved outputs
history.subtitle: Recent questionnaire answers and gap reports stored for this organisation.
history.empty: No generated outputs have been saved yet.

workbench.emptyBody: Generated answers will be grounded in passing controls, evidence, and active policies from {organisation}.
workbench.exportBlocked: PDF/XLSX export is disabled until every answer is approved.
sampleQuestions: Do you enforce MFA for all users?
Do you have an incident response policy and breach notification process?
How do you manage vendor security risk?
Are access reviews performed at least quarterly?
Do you retain audit logs for security events?

actionErrors.authRequired: Sign-in and an active organisation are required.
actionErrors.invalidInput: Paste questionnaire text or upload a text file.
actionErrors.missingConfig: AI questionnaires are not enabled or the provider is not configured.
actionErrors.noQuestions: No questions were found in the questionnaire.
actionErrors.rateLimit: The monthly limit of {limit} questionnaires has been reached.

unsupported.summary: No supported compliance context is available for this organisation yet.
unsupported.answer: No answer can be generated from the current Splnit.eu organisation data.
unsupported.notes: Add passing controls, evidence, active policies, or reviewed legal citations before using this answer externally.

complianceReport.title: Cybersecurity posture assessment report
complianceReport.dashboardBody: Export the current state of measures, evidence, and gaps to a PDF for an auditor or regulatory review.
complianceReport.controlsBody: Export the current state of measures and evidence to a PDF for an auditor or regulatory review.
complianceReport.error: The report could not be prepared.

apiKeyBlockedDetails.insufficient_scope: The saved key does not have the minimum required permissions. Check the key scopes and enter a new key.
apiKeyBlockedDetails.invalid_key: The provider rejected the saved key. Enter a new or corrected API key.
apiKeyBlockedDetails.not_connected: The API key is not connected yet. Connect the connector or document the measure manually.
apiKeyBlockedDetails.unreachable: The provider service could not be reached. Retry the check or document the measure manually.

queued.label: Queued
queued.detail: Waiting for the next collection run.

pass.label: Confirmed pass
pass.detail: Latest confirmed evidence passed this check.

gap.label: Confirmed gap
gap.detail: Latest confirmed evidence shows a gap.

blocked.label: Blocked
blocked.detail: Collection is blocked and needs attention.

activation.preservedPass: Last confirmed result is still passing while collection is blocked.

page.signedOutTitle: Draft your register from connected systems
page.signedOutSubtitle: Sign in with an active workspace to review discovered assets and suppliers.
page.disabledSubtitle: This rollout is gated while migrations and live connector smokes are completed.
page.title: Review your draft asset and supplier register
page.subtitle: Splnit.eu reads connected systems into a staging area. Confirmed rows become real register entries; dismissed rows stay out of compliance evidence.

draftNotice.title: Drafts only — you stay in control.
draftNotice.body: Connected systems can propose assets and suppliers, but only your confirmation creates register rows and evidence.

cia.title: How to read C/I/A
cia.body: C/I/A is a quick impact estimate for an asset. It is not a legal conclusion; confirm the value based on how important the asset is to your operations and customer proof.

levelDescriptions.high: material impact on customers, data, or operations
levelDescriptions.medium: important, but with limited blast radius

nextSteps.title: What to do next
nextSteps.body: Start with items you recognise. Confirming creates a real register row, dismissing removes the proposal, and uncertain rows can stay pending.
nextSteps.confirmBody: Use this when the name, owner, and impact look right for your workspace.
nextSteps.dismissBody: Use this for technical noise, irrelevant internal apps, or proposals that should not enter the register.
nextSteps.leaveBody: Nothing becomes register evidence until you confirm it. You can come back later.

empty.assets: No proposed assets are waiting for review.
empty.vendors: No proposed suppliers are waiting for review.

supplyType.SaaS application with tenant data access: SaaS application with tenant data access

assetReasons.outageDependents: outage would affect roughly {count} dependent accounts or services
assetReasons.internetFacing: internet-facing, which raises the minimum impact
assetReasons.noElevatingSignals: No elevating signals found; defaulted to low. Confirm manually.

vendorRationales.thirdPartyApp: Third-party application granted access to the Microsoft 365 tenant; review it as a candidate supplier or data processor.
